title: One-Pot Crispy Broccoli Rice & Sweet Soy Cod
summary: Fluffy rice, tender cod and crispy broccoli all cooked in one pot with a sticky, sweet soy glaze. Zero faff, maximum flavour.

method.description: Add the honey, oyster sauce and dark soy sauce to a bowl. Stir together, then add the cod fillets and mix to coat evenly. Set aside.
Wash the rice under cold water until it runs clear.

Finely grate the garlic. Trim and separate the broccoli stems from the florets — cut the florets into 2–3cm chunks, and thinly slice the stems. Trim and thinly slice the spring onions, separating the whites from the greens. Pick the coriander leaves and thinly slice the stems. Place the leaves into a bowl of ice-cold water to keep them fresh.

Place a large frying pan with a lid over a medium-high heat. Once hot, add the sesame oil and broccoli stems. Cook for 4–6 mins until softened. Add the spring onion whites, coriander stems and garlic, then stir until fragrant.
Tip in the washed rice and pour in the water. Season with salt. Let it come to the boil, then cover with a lid, reduce the heat to low and leave to steam for 12 mins.
After 12 mins, add the broccoli florets and cod fillets on top of the rice. Replace the lid and cook for another 10 mins on low.
Heat the grill to high. When the fish is done steaming, remove the lid and transfer the pan under the grill for 3–4 mins until the fish has started to colour and is cooked through.
Fluff the rice with a fork and spoon onto plates. Top with the grilled cod, then finish with sesame seeds, spring onion greens and coriander leaves.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
